commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds a Skill Workshop agent-tool guard that rejects create/revise proposal content targeting existing workspace skill paths, updates tool/docs copy, and adds focused guard/tool tests.

PR surface: Source +89, Tests +183, Docs +10. Total +282 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main's agent tool create path passes proposal markdown to proposeCreateSkill without an existing-skill reference scan; this read-only review did not run a live MCP reproduction.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow docs-only repair can align the public Skill Workshop page with the PR's intended tool-only behavior without changing runtime scope.

target.title: fix(skills): guard workshop tool creates from existing targets
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes #94947.

The built-in `skill_workshop` agent tool could accept `action=create` proposals whose markdown clearly targeted existing workspace skill paths. Applying that proposal created an unintended sibling skill instead of routing the work through the existing skill update path.

## Why This Change Was Made

This keeps the fix scoped to the reported agent-tool path. Before `action=create`, and before revising a pending create proposal, `skill_workshop` now scans `proposal_content` for workspace-relative `skills/...` path references and rejects the call when those paths resolve inside discovered workspace skill directories.

The guard uses the existing workspace skill loader with the active OpenClaw config, so direct skills, grouped skills such as `skills/group/nested-skill/SKILL.md`, non-canonical directory names, and configured trusted workspace symlink roots are checked against the actual loaded skill roots.

Shared workshop service callers such as CLI, Gateway, and autocapture are left unchanged in this PR to keep the behavior radius narrow.

## User Impact

Agents using `skill_workshop` now get an immediate, actionable error when `action=create` content points at existing workspace skills. The tool schema and Skill Workshop docs tell agents to use `action=update` with `skill_name` and split multi-skill changes into separate update proposals.

## Evidence

- Behavior addressed: the built-in `skill_workshop` tool now rejects both initial create calls and later create-proposal revisions when proposal content names an existing workspace skill path, including direct skills, grouped/nested skills, non-canonical skill directory names, and configured trusted workspace symlink skill roots.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw worktree with the repo's Codex-worktree Vitest wrapper, plus a redacted terminal proof that calls the real `createSkillWorkshopTool(...).execute(...)` handler against a temporary workspace with `skills/` symlinked to a configured trusted target.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `pnpm install`; `pnpm docs:list`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/skills/workshop/create-target-guard.test.ts src/agents/tools/skill-workshop-tool.test.ts`; `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 docs/tools/skill-workshop.md src/agents/tools/skill-workshop-tool.ts src/agents/tools/skill-workshop-tool.test.ts src/skills/workshop/create-target-guard.ts src/skills/workshop/create-target-guard.test.ts`; `base=$(git merge-base origin/main HEAD); git diff --check "$base"`; `node --import tsx` one-off script importing `createSkillWorkshopTool` and exercising the redacted terminal proof below.
- Evidence after fix: `create-target-guard.test.ts` builds real workspace skill fixtures under `skills/foo/SKILL.md`, `skills/group/nested-skill/SKILL.md`, and `skills/foo_bar/SKILL.md` and verifies existing-skill path refs are rejected while unrelated/new/url/prose refs are allowed. `skill-workshop-tool.test.ts` verifies the public `skill_workshop` wrapper rejects direct existing-skill refs for create and revise, preserves a safe create proposal without advancing it after rejected revise, and preserves configured trusted symlink behavior for both create and revise.
- Observed result after fix: the focused Vitest command passed in 2 shards covering 2 files / 19 tests; diff whitespace and oxfmt checks passed; the real tool proof below shows create/revise rejection, a safe create, and unchanged pending proposal version after rejected revise.
- Real `skill_workshop` terminal proof, with temporary paths redacted:

```text
real skill_workshop proof: configured workspace skills symlink target
setup: workspace=<tmp>/workspace, skills -> <tmp>/trusted-skills, allowSymlinkTargets=[<tmp>/trusted-skills]
create existing target: rejected: action=create cannot propose changes to existing workspace skills: skills/shared-skill/. Use action=update with skill_name for each existing skill instead.
create safe proposal: ok: {"id":"new-helper-20260620-e2562df5b6","status":"pending","kind":"create","skillName":"New Helper","skillKey":"new-helper","proposalFile":"PROPOSAL.md","supportFileCount":0,"targetSkillFile":"<tmp>/workspace/skills/new-helper/SKILL.md","scanState":"clean","proposedVersion":"v1"}
revise toward existing target: rejected: action=create cannot propose changes to existing workspace skills: skills/shared-skill/. Use action=update with skill_name for each existing skill instead.
post-revise inspect: status=pending version=v1
```

- What was not tested: no full Testbox gate or live end-to-end agent session was run; this is now a narrow Skill Workshop tool/parser/docs patch covered by focused unit/tool behavior tests, redacted terminal proof through the real tool handler, and GitHub's external-PR proof check.
- Review proof: one Codex autoreview round found two P2 issues before this draft PR; both were fixed. A later ClawSweeper review requested config-aware trusted symlink coverage plus real tool-path output; this update keeps both while narrowing the runtime behavior to the reported tool path. Per local OpenClaw guardrails, no second Codex autoreview loop was run for draft PR work.
target.author: monkeyleet

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: MonkeyLeeT
